items.scenario: You stream a model's tokens through a generator, then try to loop over the same generator again to log the full text, but get nothing.
items.question: Why, and how do you fix it?
items.answer: Generators are single-pass and were already exhausted by the first loop. Accumulate the chunks into a string/list during the first pass and reuse that, or wrap the source so you can recreate the generator when you need a second pass.

items.scenario: Embedding a 10 GB JSONL file crashes because you did data = [json.loads(line) for line in open(path)].
items.question: How do you avoid the memory blow-up?
items.answer: Stream it with a generator: process one line at a time, e.g. `for line in open(path): record = json.loads(line); embed(record)`. This keeps only one record in memory at a time instead of the whole file.

items.scenario: You call len() on a generator to size a progress bar and get a TypeError.
items.question: What's going on and what are the options?
items.answer: Generators have no length because items aren't precomputed. Either track a known total separately (e.g. from a count query), convert to a list when the data is small enough (list(gen)), or use an indeterminate progress indicator.
